extra.reasoning:
The property (never rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true) HOLDS over the entire input space.

Per-output analysis:
- rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by r2, which requires the full 12-way conjunction (diabetes, asthma, low_ejection_fraction, anticoagulated, elderly, hepatic_impairment, immunosuppressed, hypertension, polypharmacy, renal_impairment, qt_prolongation, peptic_ulcer). No other rule sets rec_drug_a, and the default is false. So rec_drug_a = true implies ALL 12 of those conditions are true.
- Therefore the only way to risk a violation is the input where all 12 conditions hold (g6pd irrelevant). In that input, consider rec_drug_b: r1 (priority 90) matches the same 12-way conjunction and sets rec_drug_b := false; r4 (priority 50) matches (diabetes true) and sets rec_drug_b := true. Conflict resolution is per-output highest-priority-wins. 90 > 50, so r1 wins and rec_drug_b := false (no tie).
- Hence whenever rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b = false. Whenever rec_drug_a = false (any input not satisfying all 12), the conjunction is trivially false regardless of rec_drug_b.

The high priority (90) of r1 was designed to exactly dominate r4 (50) in the precise and only state where rec_drug_a can be true, so rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b can never both be true. No counterexample exists.
